session.user: branch name incorrect. branch should be what the workflw run targeted
session.assistant: I can see the issue - the "branch" column in the Runs tab shows "master" for every run, but it should show the branch that each workflow run targeted. Let me explore the codebase to understand how this data is fetched and displayed.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 670 chars]...ble from the GitHub API.
Be thorough - trace from the TUI display back to the data fetch.

session.tool_result:
Now I have enough information. Let me write a comprehensive summary of my findings:
## Summary: Runs Tab Branch Column Data Flow
Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, here's the complete data flow and findings:
### 1. **Data Flow: GitHub API → RunRowData → UI Display**
**Source: GitHub REST API** (`/repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{id}/runs`) - The `gh api` command returns workflow run data including the `head_branch` field - This is a standard GitHub Actions REST API field
**Processing in `RealPlanDataProvider.fetch_runs()`** (src/erk/tui/data/real_provider.py, lines 179-325) - Calls `self._ctx.github.list_workflow_runs()` at line 197-201 - This populates a list of `WorkflowRun` objects from the GitHub API response
**Gateway Layer: `RealGitHub.list_workflow_runs()`** (packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/real.py, lines 573-615) - Uses `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/workflows/{workflow}/runs` - Maps REST API response to `WorkflowRun` objects - **Critically**: At line 608, populates `branch=run["head_branch"]` from the API response - This is the canonical source of branch data from GitHub
**RunRowData Construction** (src/erk/tui/data/real_provider.py, lines 303-324) - Line 303: `branch = run.branch if hasattr(run, "branch") else "-"` - This checks if the `WorkflowRun` object has a `branch` attribute - If present, uses `run.branch`; otherwise falls back to "-"
**Table Display** (src/erk/tui/widgets/run_table.py, lines 76 and 135) - Line 76: Column defined as `self.add_column("branch", key="branch", width=30)` - Line 135: Cell value is `row.branch` directly (no further formatting)
... [80 lines omitted] ...
- Raises `AttributeError` if the value is a `_NotAvailable` sentinel
session.assistant: Let me read the key files to understand the exact issue.

270→ 271→ # Format submission time 272→ submitted_display = strip_rich_markup(format_submission_time(run.created_at)) 273→ 274→ # Format PR-related columns 275→ pr_url: str | None = None 276→ pr_title: str | None = None 277→ pr_display = "-" 278→ title_display = "-" 279→ checks_display = "-" 280→ 281→ if pr_num is not None: 282→ pr_info = pr_info_map.get(pr_num) 283→ if pr_info is not None: 284→ graphite_url = self._ctx.graphite.get_graphite_url( 285→ GitHubRepoId(pr_info.owner, pr_info.repo), pr_info.number 286→ ) 287→ pr_url = graphite_url if use_graphite and graphite_url else pr_info.url 288→ pr_display = f"#{pr_info.number}" 289→ pr_title = pr_info.title 290→ title = pr_info.title or "-" 291→ if len(title) > _MAX_TITLE_LENGTH: 292→ title = title[: _MAX_TITLE_LENGTH - 3] + "..." 293→ title_display = title 294→ checks_display = strip_rich_markup(format_checks_cell(pr_info)) 295→ else: 296→ # Have PR number but no details 297→ pr_url = ( 298→ f"https://github.com/{self._location.repo_id.owner}/" 299→ f"{self._location.repo_id.repo}/pull/{pr_num}"
... [30 lines omitted] ...

573→ def list_workflow_runs( 574→ self, repo_root: Path, workflow: str, limit: int = 50, *, user: str | None = None 575→ ) -> list[WorkflowRun]: 576→ """List workflow runs for a specific workflow.""" 577→ # GH-API-AUDIT: REST - GET actions/workflows/{id}/runs 578→ query_params = f"per_page={limit}" 579→ if user is not None: 580→ query_params += f"&actor={user}" 581→ 582→ cmd = [ 583→ "gh", 584→ "api", 585→ f"repos/{{owner}}/{{repo}}/actions/workflows/{workflow}/runs?{query_params}", 586→ "--jq", 587→ ".workflow_runs", 588→ ] 589→ 590→ stdout = execute_gh_command_with_retry(cmd, repo_root, self._time) 591→ 592→ # Parse JSON response 593→ data = json.loads(stdout) 594→ 595→ # Map to WorkflowRun dataclasses 596→ runs = [] 597→ for run in data: 598→ # Parse created_at timestamp if present 599→ created_at = None 600→ created_at_str = run.get("created_at") 601→ if created_at_str: 602→ created_at = datetime.fromisoformat(created_at_str.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
... [20 lines omitted] ...

271→class WorkflowRun: 272→ """Information about a GitHub Actions workflow run. 273→ 274→ Immutable class representing workflow run data. Some fields may not be 275→ available depending on the API query used to fetch the data: 276→ 277→ - branch: Not available from GraphQL nodes() query 278→ - display_title: Not available from GraphQL nodes() query 279→ 280→ Accessing unavailable fields raises AttributeError. 281→ """ 282→ 283→ __slots__ = ( 284→ "_run_id", 285→ "_node_id", 286→ "_status", 287→ "_conclusion", 288→ "_branch", 289→ "_head_sha", 290→ "_display_title", 291→ "_created_at", 292→ ) 293→ 294→ _run_id: str 295→ _node_id: str | None 296→ _status: WorkflowRunStatus 297→ _conclusion: WorkflowRunConclusion | None 298→ _branch: str | _NotAvailable 299→ _head_sha: str 300→ _display_title: str | None | _NotAvailable
... [60 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now I understand the issue. These are `workflow_dispatch` runs, so `head_branch` from the GitHub API is always "master" (the branch the dispatch was triggered from). The actual target branch is the one associated with the PR. Let me check what PR info is available.
session.tool_result:
179→ def fetch_runs(self) -> list[RunRowData]: 180→ """Fetch workflow runs for the Runs tab. 181→ 182→ Ports logic from erk.cli.commands.run.list_cmd._list_runs(). 183→ 184→ Returns: 185→ List of RunRowData for display, sorted by created_at descending 186→ """ 187→ # Lazy import to avoid circular dependency through erk.cli.commands.run.__init__ 188→ from erk.cli.commands.run.shared import extract_plan_number, extract_pr_number 189→ 190→ _PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT = 20 191→ _MAX_DISPLAY_RUNS = 50 192→ _MAX_TITLE_LENGTH = 50 193→ 194→ # 1. Fetch workflow runs from all registered workflows 195→ tagged_runs: list[tuple[WorkflowRun, str]] = [] 196→ for command_name, workflow_file in WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP.items(): 197→ workflow_runs = self._ctx.github.list_workflow_runs( 198→ self._location.root, workflow_file, _PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT 199→ ) 200→ for run in workflow_runs: 201→ tagged_runs.append((run, command_name)) 202→ 203→ # Deduplicate by run_id 204→ seen: dict[str, tuple[WorkflowRun, str]] = {} 205→ for pair in tagged_runs: 206→ if pair[0].run_id not in seen: 207→ seen[pair[0].run_id] = pair 208→ tagged_runs = list(seen.values())
... [65 lines omitted] ...

140→ objective_state_display: str 141→ objective_deps_display: str 142→ objective_deps_plans: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] 143→ objective_next_node_display: str 144→ updated_at: datetime 145→ updated_display: str 146→ created_at: datetime 147→ created_display: str 148→ author: str 149→ is_learn_plan: bool 150→ lifecycle_display: str 151→ status_display: str 152→ 153→ 154→@dataclass(frozen=True) 155→class RunRowData: 156→ """Row data for displaying a workflow run in the TUI Runs tab. 157→ 158→ Contains pre-formatted display strings and raw data needed for actions. 159→ Immutable to ensure table state consistency. 160→ 161→ Attributes: 162→ run_id: GitHub Actions workflow run ID string. 163→ run_url: URL to the GitHub Actions run page. None if unavailable. 164→ status: Raw run status ("queued", "in_progress", "completed"). 165→ conclusion: Raw run conclusion ("success", "failure", "cancelled"). None if in progress. 166→ status_display: Pre-formatted status string for table display. 167→ workflow_name: Workflow command name (e.g., "plan-implement", "pr-address"). 168→ pr_number: Linked PR number. None if no PR. 169→ pr_url: URL to the linked PR. None if no PR.
... [30 lines omitted] ...
session.assistant: Let me check what fields `PullRequestInfo` has - specifically whether it includes the head branch.
session.tool_result: Found 2 files tests/test_utils/builders.py packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py

210:class PullRequestInfo: 211- """Information about a GitHub pull request.""" 212- 213- number: int 214- state: str # "OPEN", "MERGED", "CLOSED" 215- url: str 216- is_draft: bool 217- title: str | None 218- checks_passing: bool | None # None if no checks, True if all pass, False if any fail 219- owner: str # GitHub repo owner (e.g., "schrockn") 220- repo: str # GitHub repo name (e.g., "erk") 221- # True if CONFLICTING, False if MERGEABLE, None if UNKNOWN or not fetched 222- has_conflicts: bool | None = None 223- checks_counts: tuple[int, int] | None = None # (passing, total) or None if no checks 224- # True if PR will close the linked issue when merged (via "Closes #N" keywords) 225- will_close_target: bool = False 226- # Head branch name (the source branch) - optional, populated by some API calls 227- head_branch: str | None = None 228- # Review thread counts: (resolved_count, total_count) or None if not fetched 229- review_thread_counts: tuple[int, int] | None = None 230- # Review decision: "APPROVED", "CHANGES_REQUESTED", "REVIEW_REQUIRED", or None 231- review_decision: str | None = None 232- # Base branch name (the target branch) - optional, populated by some API calls 233- base_ref_name: str | None = None 234- 235- 236-class _NotAvailable: 237- """Sentinel for fields not available from certain API queries. 238- 239- Raises AttributeError when accessed as a string, preventing silent bugs
... [21 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: `PullRequestInfo` has `head_branch: str | None` at line 227. Now I need to check whether it's populated when fetched for the runs tab. Let me check how `get_prs_linked_to_issues` works and whether `head_branch` is included.
session.tool_result: 225: # WHY GRAPHQL: Used by get_prs_linked_to_issues for erk dash batch queries 226- cmd = ["gh", "api", "graphql", "-f", f"query={query}"] 227- stdout = execute_gh_command_with_retry(cmd, repo_root, self._time) 228- return json.loads(stdout) -- 743: def get_prs_linked_to_issues( 744- self, 745- location: GitHubRepoLocation, 746- plan_numbers: list[int],

743→ def get_prs_linked_to_issues( 744→ self, 745→ location: GitHubRepoLocation, 746→ plan_numbers: list[int], 747→ ) -> dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]]: 748→ """Get PRs linked to issues via CrossReferencedEvent timeline. 749→ 750→ Uses GraphQL CrossReferencedEvent to find all PRs that reference each plan, 751→ regardless of whether they will close the issue when merged. Includes open, 752→ closed, and draft PRs. Used by erk dash for batch queries with full PR data 753→ (CI status, mergeability). 754→ 755→ For simpler single-issue queries, see GitHubIssues.get_prs_referencing_issue(). 756→ 757→ Note: Uses try/except as an acceptable error boundary for handling gh CLI 758→ availability and authentication. We cannot reliably check gh installation 759→ and authentication status a priori without duplicating gh's logic. 760→ """ 761→ if not plan_numbers: 762→ return {} 763→ 764→ try: 765→ # Build and execute GraphQL query to fetch all issues 766→ query = self._build_issue_pr_linkage_query(plan_numbers, location.repo_id) 767→ response = self._execute_batch_pr_query(query, location.root) 768→ 769→ # Parse response and build inverse mapping 770→ return self._parse_issue_pr_linkages(response, location.repo_id) 771→ except (RuntimeError, FileNotFoundError, json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, IndexError): 772→ # gh not installed, not authenticated, or parsing failed
... [50 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: The GraphQL query fetches `headRefName` at line 65. Now let me check how `_parse_issue_pr_linkages` maps this to `PullRequestInfo`.
session.tool_result:
819→ def _parse_issue_pr_linkages( 820→ self, response: dict[str, Any], repo_id: GitHubRepoId 821→ ) -> dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]]: 822→ """Parse GraphQL response from issue timeline query. 823→ 824→ Processes CrossReferencedEvent timeline items to extract all PRs that 825→ reference each plan. 826→ 827→ Args: 828→ response: GraphQL response data 829→ repo_id: GitHub repository identity (owner and repo name) 830→ 831→ Returns: 832→ Mapping of plan_number -> list of PRs sorted by created_at descending 833→ """ 834→ result: dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]] = {} 835→ repo_data = response.get("data", {}).get("repository", {}) 836→ 837→ # Iterate over aliased issue results 838→ for key, issue_data in repo_data.items(): 839→ # Skip non-issue aliases or missing issues 840→ if not key.startswith("issue_") or issue_data is None: 841→ continue 842→ 843→ # Extract plan number from alias 844→ plan_number = int(key.removeprefix("issue_")) 845→ 846→ # Collect PRs with timestamps for sorting 847→ prs_with_timestamps: list[tuple[PullRequestInfo, str]] = [] 848→
... [50 lines omitted] ...
881→ elif rollup_state in ("FAILURE", "ERROR"):

899→ has_conflicts = True 900→ elif mergeable == "MERGEABLE": 901→ has_conflicts = False 902→ 903→ # Extract head branch (source branch) for landing 904→ head_ref_name = source.get("headRefName") 905→ 906→ # Note: title and labels not fetched (not needed for dash) 907→ pr_info = PullRequestInfo( 908→ number=pr_number, 909→ state=state, 910→ url=url, 911→ is_draft=is_draft if is_draft is not None else False, 912→ title=None, # Not fetched for efficiency 913→ checks_passing=checks_passing, 914→ owner=repo_id.owner, 915→ repo=repo_id.repo, 916→ has_conflicts=has_conflicts, 917→ checks_counts=checks_counts, 918→ head_branch=head_ref_name, 919→ ) 920→ 921→ # Store with timestamp for sorting 922→ if created_at: 923→ prs_with_timestamps.append((pr_info, created_at)) 924→ 925→ # Sort by created_at descending and store 926→ if prs_with_timestamps: 927→ prs_with_timestamps.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True) 928→ result[plan_number] = [pr for pr, _ in prs_with_timestamps]
... [20 lines omitted] ...
940→ 2. Failed completed runs (failures are more actionable than successes) 944→ errors gracefully.
session.assistant:
Now I have the full picture. The `PullRequestInfo.head_branch` is populated from the GraphQL `headRefName` field (line 904, 918). The fix is straightforward: instead of using `run.branch` (which is always "master" for `workflow_dispatch` runs), use `pr_info.head_branch` when available.
Let me also check how runs with direct PR numbers (not plan-linked) get their PR info fetched.
session.tool_result:
220→ # 2. Extract PR numbers and plan numbers from display_titles 221→ run_pr_numbers: dict[str, int] = {} 222→ plan_numbers: list[int] = [] 223→ 224→ for run, _workflow_name in tagged_runs: 225→ pr_num = extract_pr_number(run.display_title) 226→ if pr_num is not None: 227→ run_pr_numbers[run.run_id] = pr_num 228→ else: 229→ plan_num = extract_plan_number(run.display_title) 230→ if plan_num is not None: 231→ plan_numbers.append(plan_num) 232→ 233→ # 3. Build plan→run_ids lookup for batch PR linkage 234→ plan_to_run_ids: dict[int, list[str]] = {} 235→ for run, _wf in tagged_runs: 236→ if run.run_id in run_pr_numbers: 237→ continue 238→ run_plan = extract_plan_number(run.display_title) 239→ if run_plan is not None: 240→ plan_to_run_ids.setdefault(run_plan, []).append(run.run_id) 241→ 242→ # 4. Batch fetch PR linkages for plan-number runs 243→ pr_info_map: dict[int, PullRequestInfo] = {} 244→ if plan_numbers: 245→ pr_linkages = self._ctx.github.get_prs_linked_to_issues(self._location, plan_numbers) 246→ for plan_num, prs in pr_linkages.items(): 247→ selected_pr = select_display_pr(prs, exclude_pr_numbers=None) 248→ if selected_pr is None: 249→ continue
... [25 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Let me check the `get_pr` method and whether there's a batch PR query we can reuse.
session.tool_result:
1345→ def get_pr(self, repo_root: Path, pr_number: int) -> PRDetails | PRNotFound: 1346→ """Get comprehensive PR details via GitHub REST API. 1347→ 1348→ Uses gh api to call GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{pr_number} 1349→ which returns all PR fields in a single request. 1350→ 1351→ Returns: 1352→ PRDetails with all PR fields, or PRNotFound if PR doesn't exist 1353→ """ 1354→ assert self._repo_info is not None, "repo_info required for get_pr" 1355→ endpoint = f"/repos/{self._repo_info.owner}/{self._repo_info.name}/pulls/{pr_number}" 1356→ 1357→ # GH-API-AUDIT: REST - GET pulls/{number} 1358→ cmd = ["gh", "api", endpoint] 1359→ try: 1360→ stdout = execute_gh_command_with_retry(cmd, repo_root, self._time) 1361→ except RuntimeError: 1362→ # API call failed - PR not found or other error 1363→ return PRNotFound(pr_number=pr_number) 1364→ 1365→ data = json.loads(stdout) 1366→ return self._parse_pr_details_from_rest_api(data, self._repo_info) 1367→ 1368→ def get_pr_for_branch(self, repo_root: Path, branch: str) -> PRDetails | PRNotFound: 1369→ """Get comprehensive PR details for a branch via GitHub REST API. 1370→ 1371→ Uses gh api to call GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls?head={owner}:{branch}&state=all 1372→ which returns PRs for the branch in a single request. 1373→ 1374→ Returns:
... [15 lines omitted] ...

134:class PRDetails(EnsurableResult): 135- """Comprehensive PR information from a single GitHub API call. 136- 137- This dataclass contains all commonly-needed PR fields, allowing call sites 138- to fetch everything they need in one API call instead of multiple separate 139- calls for title, body, base branch, etc. 140- """ 141- 142- # Identity 143- number: int 144- url: str 145- 146- # Content 147- title: str 148- body: str 149- 150- # State 151- state: str # "OPEN", "MERGED", "CLOSED" 152- is_draft: bool 153- 154- # Structure 155- base_ref_name: str 156- head_ref_name: str 157- is_cross_repository: bool 158- 159- # Mergeability 160- mergeable: MergeableStatus 161- merge_state_status: str # "CLEAN", "BLOCKED", "UNSTABLE", "DIRTY" 162- 163- # Metadata
... [1 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now let me check the test for `fetch_runs` and how the fake GitHub gateway handles `get_pr_head_branches` or related methods.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Integration tests for RealPlanDataProvider.fetch_runs(). 2→ 3→Tests the fetch_runs() method which aggregates workflow runs from 4→multiple registered workflows, deduplicates, links to PRs, and 5→builds RunRowData objects for the TUI Runs tab. 6→""" 7→ 8→from datetime import UTC, datetime 9→from pathlib import Path 10→ 11→from erk.core.context import GlobalConfig 12→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 13→from erk.tui.data.real_provider import RealPlanDataProvider 14→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo 15→from erk_shared.gateway.git.fake import FakeGit 16→from erk_shared.gateway.github.fake import FakeLocalGitHub 17→from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import ( 18→ GitHubRepoId, 19→ GitHubRepoLocation, 20→ PullRequestInfo, 21→ WorkflowRun, 22→) 23→from erk_shared.gateway.graphite.fake import FakeGraphite 24→from erk_shared.gateway.http.fake import FakeHttpClient 25→from tests.fakes.context import create_test_context 26→ 27→ 28→def _make_repo_context(repo_root: Path, tmp_path: Path) -> RepoContext: 29→ """Create a RepoContext for testing.""" 30→ erk_dir = tmp_path / ".erk"
... [50 lines omitted] ...

80→ github = FakeLocalGitHub( 81→ workflow_runs=workflow_runs or [], 82→ pr_plan_linkages=pr_plan_linkages or {}, 83→ ) 84→ 85→ global_config = GlobalConfig.test(tmp_path / ".erk", use_graphite=use_graphite) 86→ 87→ ctx = create_test_context( 88→ git=git, 89→ github=github, 90→ graphite=FakeGraphite(), 91→ cwd=repo_root, 92→ global_config=global_config, 93→ repo=_make_repo_context(repo_root, tmp_path), 94→ ) 95→ 96→ location = GitHubRepoLocation( 97→ root=repo_root, 98→ repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner="test", repo="repo"), 99→ ) 100→ return RealPlanDataProvider( 101→ ctx=ctx, 102→ location=location, 103→ http_client=FakeHttpClient(), 104→ ) 105→ 106→ 107→def test_fetch_runs_empty_when_no_workflow_runs(tmp_path: Path) -> None: 108→ """fetch_runs returns empty list when no workflow runs exist.""" 109→ provider = _make_provider(tmp_path)
